questions.subject: NSEP
questions.q: A solid wooden block with a uniform cross section is floating in water (density $\rho_{\mathrm{w}}$ ) with a height $h_1$ below water. Now a flat slab of stone is placed over the wooden block but the block still floats with a height $h_2$ below water. Afterwards the stone is removed from the top and pasted at the bottom of the wooden block. The wooden block now floats with a height $h_3$ under water. Therefore, the density of the stone is

questions.subject: NSEP
questions.q:
Paragraph: A wheel of a car is made up of two parts (1)the central metal rim, and (2)the rubber tyre. The width of the tyre $W=16.5 \mathrm{~cm}$ and height $h=10.7 \mathrm{~cm}$. The rim overlaps the tyre. The total weight of the car is 1500 kg distributed evenly. The tyres are inflated with air to a pressure $2.0 \mathrm{~kg} / \mathrm{cm}^2$. The density of air at pressure of $1.0 \mathrm{~kg} / \mathrm{cm}^2$ and at room temperature equals 1.29 g /litre. The outer diameter of the tyre is 55.4 cm and that of the rim is 40 cm . Ignore the thickness of rubber and use the dimensions given here. Note that the units mentioned above are conventional units used in everyday life.
Question: The left side front tyre was observed to be in contact with the road over a length $L \mathrm{~cm}$. The value of $L$ is

questions.subject: NSEP
questions.q:
Paragraph: A wheel of a car is made up of two parts (1)the central metal rim, and (2)the rubber tyre. The width of the tyre $W=16.5 \mathrm{~cm}$ and height $h=10.7 \mathrm{~cm}$. The rim overlaps the tyre. The total weight of the car is 1500 kg distributed evenly. The tyres are inflated with air to a pressure $2.0 \mathrm{~kg} / \mathrm{cm}^2$. The density of air at pressure of $1.0 \mathrm{~kg} / \mathrm{cm}^2$ and at room temperature equals 1.29 g /litre. The outer diameter of the tyre is 55.4 cm and that of the rim is 40 cm . Ignore the thickness of rubber and use the dimensions given here. Note that the units mentioned above are conventional units used in everyday life.
Question: Consider the following two statements about a tyre of a car. Statement A: 'The horizontal road surface is exactly tangential to the tyre.' Statement B: 'The tyre is inflated with excess pressure.' Which of the following alternatives is correct?
options: Statement A is the result of statement B.
Neither of the statements A and B is true.

questions.subject: NSEP
questions.q: A home aquarium partly filled with water slides down an inclined plane of inclination angle $\theta$ with respect to the horizontal. The surface of water in the aquarium
options: remains parallel to the plane of the incline.
forms an angle $\alpha$ with the horizon where $0 < \alpha < \theta$
forms an angle $\alpha$ with horizon, where $\theta < \alpha < 90$
